problem: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue?
mechanism: Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations
hypothesis: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Optogenetic. Optogenetic control of specific neuron populations directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 12 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: To know that you do not know is the best. — Lao Tzu
